UX Researcher — Seattle, WA

Hi, I'm Brendan Thatcher.

I'm a qualitative UX Researcher who turns complex user behavior into clear product decisions — for cloud platforms, healthcare tools, and the products people use in their everyday lives.

View Projects Contact Me
6+ Years of UX Research

From cloud platforms to CPAP patients, I research real humans with real problems.

3 Industries

Enterprise cloud, healthcare, and environmental conservation.

Measurable Outcomes

Every study I run is connected to a business goal and a result.

Where I Am Now

Currently at Orcasound

Open to New Opportunities

I'm a qualitative UX Researcher and Product Manager at Orcasound, where I lead discovery research to define what gets built — and why. My work spans enterprise cloud platforms, healthcare, and environmental conservation, and I bring the same disciplined approach to all of them: understand real users, surface what matters, and make sure the product team can act on it.

I'm currently open to UX Research roles where I can combine strategic research leadership with hands-on study execution — at companies building products that improve how people work, stay healthy, or experience the world.

Featured Projects

Research That Moved Products Forward

Optum

Improving an Internal Cloud Platform to Save Software Engineers 20 Minutes a Day

A poorly designed internal tool was costing engineers time and driving them toward external resources that exposed proprietary data.

Methods: User Interviewing, Usability Testing, Contextual Inquiry
Impact
20 min saved · 3× daily sessions · 7× task success · 13 features added to roadmap
Full write-up in progressThe detailed case study is being prepared and will be available here soon.
Case study coming soon →

ResMed

Helping 166,000 CPAP Patients Stay on Therapy

CPAP patients weren't meeting insurance compliance requirements — and a new onboarding concept needed validation before launch.

Methods: Qualitative Usability Testing, Concept Testing
Impact
2% increase in 90-day therapy engagement — ~166,000 patients
Full write-up in progressThe detailed case study is being prepared and will be available here soon.
Case study coming soon →

Orcasound

Launching Shipnoise: Research-Driven Tool to Quiet Commercial Ships for Orca Conservation

No tool existed to help commercial ship Pilots understand or reduce the underwater noise their vessels create for endangered orcas.

Methods: User Interviewing, Persona Development, Journey Mapping, Workshop Moderation
Impact
Defined end user, product feature, and full design spec for MVP — currently in development
Full write-up in progressThe detailed case study is being prepared and will be available here soon.
Case study coming soon →
